---
name: Doctrinal issue
about: Disagreement on a doctrinal mapping or epistemic labeling
title: "[DOCTRINE] "
labels: doctrine
assignees: ''
---

## Location

Which assertion(s) or mapping(s) are concerned.

- File : `sifrei_yesod/sefarim/...`
- ID(s) : EC-... / Z-...
- Epistemic level currently asserted : E?

## Primary source citation

Cite the primary text(s) supporting your view. Include :

- Edition (e.g. Sefaria Mantua 1558, Vital Warsaw 1891, Ashlag Jerusalem 1955).
- Folio or section reference.
- Original-language quote (Aramaic / Hebrew).
- Translation.

## Disagreement

Explain the disagreement. Typical categories :

- Mistranslation or over-translation.
- Incorrect epistemic level (e.g. E1 claimed but actually E3 derivation).
- Attribution error (e.g. Vital's reading labeled as Zohar).
- Missing `divergence_note` when sources diverge.
- Synthesis that crosses traditions (e.g. Kabbalah + Sufism) without warrant.

## Proposed resolution

What the corrected mapping / label should look like.

## Philological context

Any additional editorial context (manuscript variants, Nusah Aher, commentator disputes) relevant to the resolution.
